name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 04.05.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 04.05.2026.pdf

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 04.05.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 04.05.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on May 01, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 81.60 lakh ha as compared to 79.00 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 31.05 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.
texts.text:  As on May 01, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 81.60 lakh ha as compared to 79.00 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 31.05 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 01.05.2026, 95.25% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. 100% area of Pulses has been completed; 99.87% area of Oilseeds majorly in Rapeseed & Mustard (100%); 96.22% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (98.86%), 59.32% area of Rice and 97.00% area of Wheat has been harvested for rabi crops 2025-26.
texts.text:  As on 01.05.2026, 95.25% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. 100% area of Pulses has been completed; 99.87% area of Oilseeds majorly in Rapeseed & Mustard (100%); 96.22% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (98.86%), 59.32% area of Rice and 97.00% area of Wheat has been harvested for rabi crops 2025-26.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  West Rajasthan,  some  parts  of Saurashtra-Kutch and some parts of Odisha.
texts.text: Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  West Rajasthan,  some  parts  of Saurashtra-Kutch and some parts of Odisha.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  May  26  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  4% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 17%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 9%; (iii) Central India: 31%; and South Peninsula: (-) 23%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  May  26  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  4% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 17%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 9%; (iii) Central India: 31%; and South Peninsula: (-) 23%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall along with thunderstorm, lightning and gusty winds with heavy rainfall likely over Sub Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim and northeast India during 03rd 05th May.
texts.text:  Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall along with thunderstorm, lightning and gusty winds with heavy rainfall likely over Sub Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim and northeast India during 03rd 05th May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated to scattered thunderstorms, lightning & gusty winds and hailstorm likely over Northwest, central and East India during 03rd-05th May, 2026.
texts.text:  Isolated to scattered thunderstorms, lightning & gusty winds and hailstorm likely over Northwest, central and East India during 03rd-05th May, 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  to  scattered  thunderstorms,  lightning  &  gusty  winds  and  isolated  heavy  rainfall  likely Tamil Nadu, Puducherry & Karaikal, South Interior Karnataka, Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam and Kerala & Mahe during the week.
texts.text:  Isolated  to  scattered  thunderstorms,  lightning  &  gusty  winds  and  isolated  heavy  rainfall  likely Tamil Nadu, Puducherry & Karaikal, South Interior Karnataka, Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam and Kerala & Mahe during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Widespread rainfall activity likely over Northeast India and adjoining East India.
texts.text:  Widespread rainfall activity likely over Northeast India and adjoining East India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  to  Scattered  rainfall  activity  likely  over  North  India  and  Southern  parts  of  Peninsular India.
texts.text:  Isolated  to  Scattered  rainfall  activity  likely  over  North  India  and  Southern  parts  of  Peninsular India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Hot  &  humid  weather  conditions  very  likely  to  prevail  in  isolated  pockets  over  Tamil  Nadu, Puducherry & Karaikal and Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam on 04th May and over Konkan & Goa during 5th -7th May.
texts.text:  Hot  &  humid  weather  conditions  very  likely  to  prevail  in  isolated  pockets  over  Tamil  Nadu, Puducherry & Karaikal and Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam on 04th May and over Konkan & Goa during 5th -7th May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  30  April  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 71.082 billion cubic meter, which is 38.72% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 114.10 percent of last  year's  storage  and  126.53  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  30  April  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 71.082 billion cubic meter, which is 38.72% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 114.10 percent of last  year's  storage  and  126.53  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text:  Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.
texts.text: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During  the  week  (23  April  -  29  April  2026),  lower  rainfall  was  observed,  as  compared  to  Long Period Average (LPA) by 46%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 29 April 2026) is lower than LPA by 4%.
texts.text:  During  the  week  (23  April  -  29  April  2026),  lower  rainfall  was  observed,  as  compared  to  Long Period Average (LPA) by 46%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 29 April 2026) is lower than LPA by 4%.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: a) RainFall figures are based on operation data.
texts.text: a) RainFall figures are based on operation data.

texts.label: footnote
texts.orig: b) Small figures indicate actual rainfal (mm), while bold figures indicate Normal rainfall (mm).
texts.text: b) Small figures indicate actual rainfal (mm), while bold figures indicate Normal rainfall (mm).

texts.label: footnote
texts.orig: c) Percentage Departures of rainfall are shown in brackets.
texts.text: c) Percentage Departures of rainfall are shown in brackets.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, thunderstorm activity is likely to continue over the northwest, east & northeast, and south peninsular India during many days of the week.
texts.text: Overall, thunderstorm activity is likely to continue over the northwest, east & northeast, and south peninsular India during many days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall is likely to be normal to above normal over most parts of the country.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall is likely to be normal to above normal over most parts of the country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 2-3°C likely over Northwest till 02nd May; gradual fall by 3-5°C during 03st -06th May.
texts.text:  Gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 2-3°C likely over Northwest till 02nd May; gradual fall by 3-5°C during 03st -06th May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual fall in maximum temperatures by about 2°C likely over Central India till 01st May and no significant change during 02nd -06th May.
texts.text:  Gradual fall in maximum temperatures by about 2°C likely over Central India till 01st May and no significant change during 02nd -06th May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over rest parts of the country.
texts.text:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over rest parts of the country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Heat  wave  conditions  very  likely  in  some  places  of  Vidarbha  on  30th  April  &  01st  May;  West Rajasthan on 02nd & 03rd May.
texts.text:  Heat  wave  conditions  very  likely  in  some  places  of  Vidarbha  on  30th  April  &  01st  May;  West Rajasthan on 02nd & 03rd May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low  Probability  of  Heatwave  conditions  likely  during  some  days  of  week  2  over  Rajasthan  & Vidarbha,  and  adjoining  southern  parts  of  Madhya  Pradesh  and  Chhattisgarh.  Above-normal maximum temperatures (+1.6 to +3.0°C) are likely over many parts of Rajasthan and Gujarat, along the  west  coast,  and  over  Odisha  &  Coastal  Andhra  Pradesh  on  the  east  coast.  Below-normal maximum temperatures (-1.6 to -3.0°C) are likely over parts of Bihar, Jharkhand, and Gangetic West Bengal. Near-normal weekly maximum temperatures are likely over the rest of the country.
texts.text:  Low  Probability  of  Heatwave  conditions  likely  during  some  days  of  week  2  over  Rajasthan  & Vidarbha,  and  adjoining  southern  parts  of  Madhya  Pradesh  and  Chhattisgarh.  Above-normal maximum temperatures (+1.6 to +3.0°C) are likely over many parts of Rajasthan and Gujarat, along the  west  coast,  and  over  Odisha  &  Coastal  Andhra  Pradesh  on  the  east  coast.  Below-normal maximum temperatures (-1.6 to -3.0°C) are likely over parts of Bihar, Jharkhand, and Gangetic West Bengal. Near-normal weekly maximum temperatures are likely over the rest of the country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No warm night conditions are likely during the week.
texts.text:  No warm night conditions are likely during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rice : About 31.05 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.42 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Rice : About 31.05 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.42 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Pulses : About 23.49 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 22.76 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Pulses : About 23.49 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 22.76 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Oilseeds : About 11.04 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 9.58 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Oilseeds : About 11.04 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 9.58 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
